hero.headline: Say the thought. See the loop.
hero.subheadline: MindMirror is an early product for voice-first reflection. Start with one thought, save it, and see the possible pattern, cost, question, and next step.
hero.social_proof: Early product access. The mirror gets sharper as you add more reflections.

f1.title: Voice-first capture
f1.body: Start with browser dictation or text. The product is being shaped around getting thoughts out quickly, not writing perfect journal entries.

f2.title: Memory that builds
f2.body: Every saved reflection becomes evidence. One entry gives a mirror; three starts early signals; ten unlocks a clearer pattern map.

f3.title: Pattern detection
f3.body: MindMirror looks for topics, emotions, repeated phrases, and decision loops so the reflection becomes useful immediately.

f4.title: Honest pattern insight
f4.body: Less vague positivity. More: 'This keeps appearing when you talk about work decisions.'

f5.title: Daily insight direction
f5.body: The long-term direction is a simple daily surface: what came up, what repeated, what it cost, and what to do next.

f6.title: Your data, forever
f6.body: Export everything anytime. Your thoughts belong to you.

social_proof.quote1: Most journaling tools help you record thoughts. MindMirror is built to show what keeps repeating.
social_proof.quote2: The first mirror turns vague overthinking into a named loop you can inspect.
social_proof.quote3: The product is early, but the core promise is live: one honest thought should give you something useful back.

pricing.title: Pricing
pricing.subtitle: Choose a plan to join the early-access waitlist. MindMirror is not a live app yet.
pricing.card_status: Early-access waitlist reservation. No live app access yet.
pricing.trial_days: {days}-day Stripe trial before billing; waitlist only today
pricing.empty_state: No plans synced yet. In Stripe, tag products with metadata foundation = true, add price lookup_key and metadata internal_id, then run npm run stripe:sync.

features.alerts: Planned pattern alerts when a topic repeats

faq.q1: Is this just another journaling app?
faq.a1: No. Most journaling apps store your thoughts. MindMirror analyzes reflections for repeated topics, emotions, phrases, and decision loops. It's the difference between a diary and a mirror.
faq.q2: How does the pattern detection work?
faq.a2: The intended product analyzes entries for topics, goals, emotions, and recurring phrases. Over time, it should connect the dots and show what keeps coming up.
faq.q3: Do I have to write or type anything?
faq.a3: No. You can use browser dictation or type. The core experience is designed around getting the thought out quickly instead of forcing a writing habit.
faq.a4: For the early-access demand test, checkout is waitlist-only and refundable where stated. Full subscription management will be handled when the live product ships.
faq.a5: Your entries are private and encrypted. We never sell or share your data. You can export and delete everything at any time.

how_it_works.title: How it works
how_it_works.subtitle: The first product moment is intentionally simple: one thought in, one useful mirror out.
how_it_works.step1_body: Dictate or type what keeps coming back. No mood survey, no long setup, no pressure to make it polished.
how_it_works.step2_body: MindMirror names the possible loop, the cost, the evidence it used, and the next reflection question.
how_it_works.step3_body: As you add reflections, MindMirror starts comparing what repeats across days instead of treating every entry as isolated.

meta.title: MindMirror
meta.description: A voice-first AI journaling product designed to detect thought patterns over time and show what keeps coming back.
meta.home_title: Say the thought. See the loop.
meta.home_description: Try MindMirror's first reflection loop: capture one thought and see the possible pattern, cost, question, and next step.
meta.pricing_description: Early-access plans for the MindMirror waitlist. The live app is still being built.
meta.privacy_description: How MindMirror handles your data and protects your privacy.
